screens.name: Audio Harmony Pipeline
screens.purpose: Convert mp3 input into a normalized lead-sheet draft with explicit uncertainty.

ux_rules: Keep extraction separate from harmonic interpretation.
Prefer conservative review-required markers over invented harmonic detail.
Preserve source uncertainty whenever meter or chord confidence is weak.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Produce a structured draft that can feed the existing theory layer without mixing extraction and interpretation.
interaction_risks: Beat tracking can over-segment the harmony.
Meter hints can be wrong on syncopated or rubato sections.
notes: No user-facing UI changes are in scope.

constraints: Do not rely on LLM inference for beat tracking or chord extraction.
Do not fail hard when the Python adapter cannot analyze a file.
open_decisions: Future work may replace template matching with a stronger MIR backend.
Future work may refine bar grouping for double-time or half-time detection.

critical_judgments: Adapter output maps cleanly into normalized measures and events.
Uncertainty remains explicit throughout the audio path.
must_not_regress: Symbolic pipeline behavior must stay unchanged.
Audio failures must not crash the orchestration layer.

visual_constraints: No visual UI changes were part of this task.
